demographics.living_situation: resident in an aged-care (nursing home) facility; normally mobilises with a frame

contact_nok.relationship: aged-care facility nursing staff (present at the encounter and making the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The nurses are calling for me — I woke up completely soaked through the bedsheets, freezing cold and shaking, and this lump on my neck has doubled in size overnight and it really hurts to touch now.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It all came on suddenly overnight — the sweats, the shivering and the neck lump getting bigger and painful — and the staff are worried it's not right for me tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: the sweats, shivering and rapid neck-lump swelling came on suddenly overnight; the tiredness has been building for about three weeks
symptom_narrative.duration: the acute symptoms started last night; the neck lump grew over about 24 hours
symptom_narrative.character: drenching night sweats that needed a full bed-linen change, shaking chills (rigors), and a neck lump that swelled quickly and became painful and hard to turn the neck with
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the staff say the patient is completely wiped out and can't get out of bed tonight because of the exhaustion and shaking
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right side of the neck for the lump; the sweats and shivering are all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started overnight and not settling
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; the nurses gave some paracetamol about an hour ago
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has just kept getting worse on its own through the night
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling full quickly when trying to eat over the past week, and feeling generally drained and lethargic
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no episode like this before; the neck lump used to be there but was small, soft and painless
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks with a frame but is bedbound tonight due to exhaustion and shaking

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a slow-growing blood cancer of the white cells (chronic leukaemia) that has been watched but not treated for about five years
past_medical_history.how_managed: watch-and-wait — no chemotherapy or tablets, just regular blood-test monitoring

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: told the immune system is weak because of the blood condition

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in an aged-care facility, frail baseline, dependent on nursing staff for care tonight

objective_data_offered.value: temperature 38.6 degrees measured on the facility thermometer overnight
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single facility-thermometer reading reported by nursing staff; paracetamol given about an hour ago may now be masking the true fever

objective_data_offered.value: a firm right-sided neck lump that staff say has visibly doubled in size overnight, is red over the skin and tender
patient visibly shivering in bed in damp sleepwear, with sweat-soaked bed linen bagged in the corner of the room
staff report the patient looks very pale and has several easy bruises on both forearms with no remembered injury
